title: Comparative: Federalism
lead: India: ==cooperative, asymmetric, centralising==. US: ==competitive, symmetric, entrenched==. UK: ==devolutionary and revocable==. France: ==unitary with administrative decentralisation==.

head: Aspect
India
United States
United Kingdom
France
rows: ==Quasi-federal== — federal features, unitary bias (Part XI, Arts 245–263).
States ==not sovereign== — exist at Parliament's will (Art 3).
Devolved powers ==granted and revocable== by Westminster.
States derive power ==from the Constitution== — not a compact.
Constitution as a ==compact among sovereign states==.
Enumerated federal powers; ==residuary with states (10th Amendment)==.
==Art 368== — some need half the states' ratification; ==no state veto==.
==Finance Commission (Art 280), GST Council==; Centre collects the bulk.
Both tax independently — ==strong state fiscal power==.
